title: Naver Mail Compose: Read React SPA state via CDP fiber props
desc: Naver mail compose fields (subject, recipient, attachment) invisible to standard a11y tree. Use --eval-js with React fiber traversal.
steps: Read subject value via eval-js on chrome domain mail.naver.com: Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('input,textarea')).find(e=>{var r=e.getBoundingClientRect();return r.width>50&&(e.type==='text'

requirements.expect: returns non-empty subject value from Naver mail compose
requirements.prompt: React fiber CDP eval skill documented --skill naver-mail-compose-spa-read-fiber --commit a0f0219071c4255e3a0437e99b17bf23a7e873fd --class skill-only

requirements.expect: returns element with recipient email address as accessible value
requirements.prompt: React fiber CDP eval skill documented --skill naver-mail-compose-spa-read-fiber --commit a0f0219071c4255e3a0437e99b17bf23a7e873fd --class skill-only

requirements.expect: attachment filename visible in a11y tree when file is attached
